docs.title: What Is SHATTERED?
docs.text:
SHATTERED is a small-scale narrative skirmish game set during the Horus Heresy.

It focuses on isolated groups of Space Marines operating after catastrophic engagements — cut off from command structures, low on ammunition, and fighting without reinforcement.

This is not a simulation of Legion warfare.

It is a game about fractured units, hard commitments, irreversible consequences and despair.

docs.title: Relationship to Horus Heresy: Age of Darkness
docs.text:
SHATTERED is designed for players familiar with Warhammer: Horus Heresy – Age of Darkness.

It preserves:

  • Space Marine statlines
  • Weapon profiles
  • Core shooting mechanics
  • Melee characteristics
  • The underlying combat math

Age of Darkness rulebooks remain useful references for:

  • Profiles
  • Wargear
  • Weapon characteristics

SHATTERED changes the structure of play — not the martial language of the setting.

The goal is to create an easy transition for Horus Heresy players, while shifting the focus from army-scale engagements to small, independent warbands.

docs.title: Design Lineage
docs.text:
Structurally, SHATTERED is inspired by Mordheim.

It adopts:

  • Independent model activation
  • Persistent injuries
  • Warband-level morale and rout tests
  • Post-battle exploration
  • Resource scarcity

Where Age of Darkness provides battlefield language and statistics, Mordheim provides sequencing and consequence.

docs.title: Scale of Play
docs.text:
A typical warband consists of:

  • 5–7 models
  • Individually activated
  • With persistent injuries and equipment

Engagements are played on dense, vertical terrain.

Positioning matters. Commitment matters. Disengagement is dangerous.

docs.title: Core Experience
docs.text:
SHATTERED prioritizes:

  • Commitment over flexibility
  • Risk over optimization
  • Fictional coherence over symmetry
  • Clarity over layered mechanics

Combat is decisive. Mistakes have consequences. Campaign progression is uncertain.

Models will be injured. Weapons will fail. Warbands will rout.

docs.title: What This Game Is Not
docs.text:
SHATTERED is not:

  • Age of Darkness at reduced points
  • A competitive tournament format
  • A reaction-driven tactical system
  • A layered stratagem engine

It is a skirmish system built for narrative campaign play within the Age of Darkness setting.

docs.title: Living Document
docs.text:
SHATTERED is under active development and playtesting.

Rules may change. Mechanics may be removed or refined.

The Git repository is the canonical source of truth.

docs.title: Design Principles
docs.text:
SHATTERED is governed by a defined set of structural priorities.

When mechanical decisions are unclear, these principles take precedence.

docs.title: 1. Structural Hierarchy
docs.text:
SHATTERED separates structure from statistics.

  • Structure and sequencing are inspired by Mordheim.
  • Statistics, profiles, and weapon characteristics are preserved from Warhammer: Horus Heresy – Age of Darkness unless explicitly overridden.

Age of Darkness statlines, weapon profiles, and core combat math remain intact by default.

Changes to statlines or weapon profiles are a last resort and only implemented when required to preserve clarity at the skirmish scale.

When structural conflict occurs:

  • Mordheim structure governs resolution.
  • Age of Darkness statistics remain intact unless explicitly modified.

docs.title: Explicit Structural Exclusions
docs.text:
The following Age of Darkness systems do not apply:

  • No Reactions.
  • No Challenges.
  • Keywords do not apply unless explicitly referenced within SHATTERED.

These exclusions prevent procedural layering and maintain clarity at the model level.

docs.title: 2. Commitment Over Flexibility
docs.text:
Actions carry risk.

  • Charging commits a model.
  • Breaking from combat is dangerous.
  • Running prevents shooting.
  • Positioning errors are punishable.

Models should not act without consequence.

docs.title: 3. Risk Over Optimization
docs.text:
The game rewards informed decisions — not list efficiency.

  • Combat is lethal.
  • Ammunition is scarce.
  • Weapons may misfire.
  • Injuries persist between battles.

Mechanics should not exist solely to smooth variance or eliminate risk.

docs.title: 4. Fictional Coherence Over Symmetry
docs.text:
Balance is desirable. Symmetry is not mandatory.

If a rule improves narrative plausibility at the cost of mechanical neatness, plausibility prevails.

docs.title: 5. Clarity Over Layered Status Effects
docs.text:
Rules resolve cleanly.

Avoid:

  • Stacking conditional modifiers
  • Nested reaction chains
  • Temporary layered statuses

If a mechanic requires multiple tracking tokens to function, it should be reconsidered.

docs.title: 6. Independent Action
docs.text:
Models act individually.

  • Each activation matters.
  • Each model’s survival matters.
  • Warband morale reflects cumulative loss.

Unit-level abstraction is intentionally avoided.

docs.title: 7. Persistence Matters
docs.text:
Campaign play is central.

  • Injuries remain.
  • Equipment must be replaced.
  • Resource Caches generate long-term advantage slowly.
  • Reinforcements are uncertain.

Progression should feel earned, not guaranteed.

docs.title: 8. Verticality Is Tactical
docs.text:
Terrain influences decisions.

  • Height offers advantage.
  • Falling is dangerous.
  • Diving charges are meaningful.
  • Movement is positional.

Boards should reward planning and punish careless exposure.

docs.title: 9. Brutality With Restraint
docs.text:
Combat resolves quickly.

A fight should not require multiple procedural layers to conclude.

When possible:

  • Roll.
  • Resolve.
  • Continue play.

docs.title: Design Authority
docs.text:
SHATTERED is not a consensus-driven document.

Playtest feedback may inform changes.

The canonical rulebook repository defines the active ruleset.

docs.title: Core Rules Overview
docs.text:
This section defines how a battle of SHATTERED is played.

Core Rules govern all in-battle procedures, including:

  • Battlefield preparation
  • Deployment
  • Model activation
  • Movement
  • Shooting
  • Melee combat
  • Morale and Rout tests
  • Vertical interaction

Campaign systems such as injuries, exploration, salvage, and reinforcements are handled separately.

docs.title: Design Hierarchy
docs.text:
SHATTERED is a Mordheim-first system.

  • Mordheim governs structure, sequencing, and resolution order.
  • Age of Darkness governs statlines, weapon profiles, and equipment characteristics unless explicitly overridden.
  • Where conflict exists, Mordheim conventions take precedence.

docs.title: Activation Model
docs.text:
SHATTERED uses traditional IGOUGO activation.

  • Players alternate activating their entire warband.
  • A player's models completes their movement and actions before control passes.
  • There are no interrupt mechanics.
  • All effects resolve immediately unless explicitly stated otherwise.
  • There are no units and no coherency requirements.

All targeting and damage resolution occur at the model level. There is no wound pooling.

docs.title: Modifiers
docs.text:
Modifiers stack unless explicitly stated otherwise.

The following shooting modifiers are established:

  • –1 to hit if a model moved and fires a non-Assault weapon.
  • –1 to hit if the target is beyond half the weapon’s maximum range.

docs.title: Structure of a Battle
docs.text:
A standard game proceeds as follows:

  1. Prepare the battlefield.
  2. Deploy warbands.
  3. Resolve turns using the Mordheim sequence of play.
  4. Test for Rout when required.
  5. End the battle when one warband withdraws or is eliminated.

Each turn follows the Mordheim sequence:

  1. Recovery Phase
  2. Movement Phase
  3. Shooting Phase
  4. Close Combat Phase
  5. Rout Phase

Each phase is defined in detail in the sections that follow.

docs.title: Rules Authority
docs.text:
Core Rules govern all in-battle resolution.

  • Mission rules override Core Rules only where explicitly stated.
  • Campaign mechanics do not alter combat procedures unless explicitly defined.
  • If a rule is not modified by a mission or campaign mechanic, the Core Rules apply as written.

docs.title: Sequence of Play
docs.text:
A battle of SHATTERED follows a defined structure.

All in-battle procedures must follow this order unless explicitly modified by a mission.

Pre-game setup (terrain, Resource Caches, deployment, and first turn determination) is resolved before the first turn begins, as defined in the Core Rules.

docs.title: Turn Structure
docs.text:
Each turn follows the Mordheim sequence:

  1. Recovery Phase
  2. Movement Phase
  3. Shooting Phase
  4. Close Combat Phase
  5. Rout Phase

Players alternate activating their warband at a time during each phase.

  • There are no reactions.
  • There are no interrupt mechanics.
  • An activated warband completes all permitted actions before control passes to the opponent.
  • All effects resolve immediately unless explicitly stated otherwise.

docs.title: 1. Recovery Phase
docs.text:
Resolve all effects that occur at the start of the turn.

  • Stunned models become Knocked Down.
  • Knocked Down models remain Knocked Down until they stand during their Movement Phase.
  • Any rule that specifies it resolves at the start of a turn is resolved here.

docs.title: 2. Movement Phase
docs.text:
Models may:

  • Move
  • Run
  • Charge
  • Jump
  • Stand Up

Movement rules and restrictions are defined in:

02-movement.md

docs.title: 3. Shooting Phase
docs.text:
Models that are eligible to shoot may resolve ranged attacks.

Eligibility, modifiers, ammunition rules, and misfires are defined in:

03-shooting.md

docs.title: 4. Close Combat Phase
docs.text:
Resolve all combats involving engaged models.

Combat sequence, charging bonuses, critical hits, and injury resolution are defined in:

04-melee.md

docs.title: 5. Rout Phase
docs.text:
At the end of the turn, check whether a warband must test for Rout.

  • If 25% or more of the warband is Out of Action, it must take a Rout Test.
  • If the test is failed, the battle ends immediately.

Rout and voluntary withdrawal are defined in:

05-leadership-morale.md

docs.title: Ending the Battle
docs.text:
A battle ends when:

  • A warband fails a Rout Test.
  • A warband voluntarily withdraws.
  • A warband is reduced to zero Operational models.
  • A mission objective declares the battle over.

Post-battle procedures are resolved in the Campaign Rules.

docs.title: Movement
docs.text:
Movement determines how models reposition across the battlefield.

All distances are measured in inches.

A model must complete its movement before performing any other action during its activation.

Vertical movement, climbing, jumping, and falling are defined in:

06-terrain-verticality.md

docs.title: 1. Normal Move
docs.text:
During its activation, a model may move up to its Movement (M) characteristic.

  • Movement may be taken in any direction.
  • A model may pivot freely during movement.
  • A model may not move through other models.
  • A model may not move through impassable terrain.
  • A model must end its move in a legal position.

docs.title: 2. Run
docs.text:
A model may choose to Run instead of making a Normal Move.

  • A Running model may move up to Movement + Initiative (M + I).
  • A model that Runs may not shoot during the same activation.
  • A model that is Engaged at the start of its activation may not Run.
  • Running does not allow a model to enter base contact with an enemy unless declaring a Charge (see Melee).

Running represents committed forward movement at speed.

docs.title: 3. Engaged Models
docs.text:
A model is Engaged when it is in base contact with an enemy model.

An Engaged model:

  • May not make a Normal Move.
  • May not Run.
  • Must resolve combat during its activation as defined in:

04-melee.md

Leaving engagement is resolved under the Melee rules.

docs.title: 4. Movement Restrictions
docs.text:
Movement must follow these constraints:

  • A model may not end its movement overlapping another model.
  • A model may not pass through enemy models.
  • Friendly models may not be moved through unless physically permitted by terrain and base sizes.
  • A model must be able to physically occupy the position it ends its movement in.

If a model cannot legally reach its declared position, the movement ends at the furthest legal point along its path.

docs.title: 5. Measuring Movement
docs.text:
Movement is measured from the edge of a model’s base to its new position.

Pre-measuring is permitted.

All measurements must be declared clearly before repositioning the model.

docs.title: Shooting
docs.text:
The Shooting Phase resolves all ranged attacks made by eligible models.

All shooting is resolved at the model level.

There is no wound pooling and no unit-based targeting.

docs.title: 1. Eligibility to Shoot
docs.text:
A model may shoot if:

  • It did not Run this activation.
  • It did not Charge this activation.
  • It is not Engaged in close combat.

Assault weapons may always fire unless explicitly prohibited by another rule.

docs.title: 2. Targeting
docs.text:
• A model must target the closest visible enemy model where applicable.
  • Line of sight must be physically traceable from the firing model to the target.
  • Cover and visibility modifiers follow Mordheim conventions.

All attacks are resolved against a single target model.

docs.title: 3. Shooting Modifiers
docs.text:
The following modifiers apply:

  • –1 to hit if the firing model moved and is using a non-Assault weapon.
  • –1 to hit if the target is beyond half of the weapon’s maximum range.

Modifiers stack unless explicitly stated otherwise.

docs.title: Cover Modifiers
docs.text:
Cover represents terrain or obstacles that partially obscure a target from incoming fire. If a target is partially hidden by terrain, the shooter suffers a –1 penalty to hit (soft cover). If the target is mostly hidden behind solid terrain, the penalty is –2 to hit (hard cover).

If a shot passes within 1" of another model, the target counts as being in soft cover (–1 to hit).

A model that cannot be seen at all cannot be targeted.

docs.title: 4. Resolving a Shot
docs.text:
To resolve a ranged attack:

  1. Declare target.
  2. Roll To Hit (BS vs required value).
  3. Roll To Wound.
  4. Resolve Armour Saves.
  5. If an unsaved wound is caused, roll on the Injury table.

Critical hits bypass armour saves.

Injury resolution is defined in:

04-melee.md (Injury Resolution section)

docs.title: 5. Ammunition & Scarcity
docs.text:
In SHATTERED, ammunition is limited, so every shot matters and running out can cost you the fight. All ranged and special weapons are subject to limited amounts of ammunition each game.

Weapons with a Firepower (FP) characteristic greater than 2 still count as a single shot.

Bolt Pistols are not subject to ammunition scarcity.

docs.title: 5.1 Ranged Weapon (Boltguns) Ammunition
docs.text:
At the start of the battle, roll 1D6 per ranged weapon (Boltguns) to determine ammunition availability.

The result applies for the duration of that battle only.

D6 Result 1 Unstable Supply – Any unmodified hit roll of 1 triggers a Weapon Misfire roll. 2–5 The weapon may fire that many times during the battle. 6 Unlimited shots for the battle.

docs.title: 5.2 Special Weapon Ammunition
docs.text:
At the start of the battle, roll 1D3 per ranged weapon to determine ammunition availability.

The result applies for the duration of that battle only.

D6 Result 1 One shot during the battle. 2 Two shots during the battle. 3 Three shots during the battle.

docs.title: 6. Special Weapons – Risk of Catastrophic Failure
docs.text:
All special weapons are unstable.

Any unmodified hit roll of 1 made with a special weapon immediately triggers a roll on the Weapon Misfire Table, regardless of ammunition availability.

This effect is in addition to any misfire triggered by an Unstable Supply result.

docs.title: 7. Weapon Misfire Table
docs.text:
When a weapon suffers a misfire, roll 1D6:

D6 Result 1 BOOM! The weapon explodes. The shooter suffers an S4 hit (no critical hit possible). The weapon is destroyed. 2 Jammed. The weapon is jammed and useless for the remainder of the battle. 3 Phut. The weapon fails to fire. The shooter must wait one extra turn before firing this weapon again. 4–5 Click. The weapon fails to fire with no additional effect. 6 KA-BOOM! The shot hits the intended target and counts as having +1 Strength.

Destroyed weapons may not be used again during the battle.

Persistent damage effects are resolved in the Campaign Rules.

docs.title: Melee
docs.text:
The Close Combat Phase resolves all fighting between Engaged models.

Combat follows Mordheim structure and sequencing.

There are no Challenges. There are no Reactions.

docs.title: 1. Engagement
docs.text:
A model is Engaged when it is in base contact with one or more enemy models.

  • A model is engaged with all enemy models it is in base contact with.
  • A model may choose which engaged enemy to direct its attacks against.
  • Facing is ignored.

docs.title: 2. Charging
docs.text:
To Charge:

  • The model declares a Charge.
  • The model moves up to Movement + Initiative".
  • The model must attempt to reach base contact with the declared target.

If the model cannot reach base contact, the Charge fails and the model stops at the furthest legal point along its path.

A model that successfully Charges:

  • Counts as charging.
  • Strikes before non-charging opponents in the first round of combat.

All models that successfully charge the same enemy count as charging and gain charging benefits.

A model may not Charge if it began its activation Engaged.

docs.title: Boarding Shield – Shield Bash
docs.text:
A model equipped with a Boarding Shield may attempt a Shield Bash when declaring a Charge.

If, during a successful Charge, the charging model would reach base contact with more than 2" of charge distance remaining, it may declare a Shield Bash before resolving combat.

The Shield Bash is resolved immediately upon reaching base contact.

docs.title: Shield Bash Procedure
docs.text:
1. Both players roll 1D6.
  2. Compare results.

docs.title: If the Charging Model Wins
docs.text:
The charging model may push the target model directly away up to 2".

  • The pushed model must move in a straight line directly away from the charger.
  • If this movement forces the model off a ledge or elevated surface, the model immediately falls.
  • Resolve falling distance and injury as normal.
  • If no fall occurs, the target model is Knocked Down.

The charging model then moves into base contact as normal.

The charge counts as successful and the charger retains charging benefits.

docs.title: If the Charging Model Loses or Ties
docs.text:
The Shield Bash fails.

  • The charging model does not gain charging strike priority.
  • The defender strikes first in the first round of combat.
  • The charge otherwise proceeds normally.

docs.title: Restrictions
docs.text:
• Shield Bash may only be attempted once per Charge.
  • Shield Bash may not be used if the charging model began its activation Engaged.
  • Shield Bash does not replace normal attacks.
  • This rule only applies to models equipped with a Boarding Shield.

docs.title: 3. Combat Sequence
docs.text:
Resolve each combat in the following order:

  1. Determine strike order (charging models strike first).
  2. Roll To Hit (Weapon Skill vs Weapon Skill).
  3. Roll To Wound.
  4. Resolve Armour Saves.
  5. Roll on the Injury table for each unsaved wound.

Each model resolves its attacks individually.

There is no wound pooling.

docs.title: 4. Critical Hits
docs.text:
Critical hits bypass armour saves.

Other effects of critical hits follow Mordheim conventions unless explicitly overridden.

docs.title: 5. Injury Resolution (In-Game)
docs.text:
When a model suffers an unsaved wound, roll 1D6:

  • 1–2: Knocked Down
  • 3–4: Stunned
  • 5–6: Out of Action

All lasting injury effects are resolved after the battle in the Campaign Rules.

docs.title: 6. Multiple Wounds
docs.text:
Multi-wound models:

  • Ignore the first Out of Action result.
  • Instead lose one Wound.
  • If reduced to 0 Wounds, further Out of Action results apply normally.

Weapons that cause multiple wounds apply +1 to the Injury roll.

docs.title: 7. Breaking from Combat
docs.text:
A model may break from combat if:

  • It panics, or
  • A rule requires it to flee.

When a model breaks from combat:

  • It immediately moves away as required.
  • The opposing model gains an automatic hit against the fleeing model.

There is no separate Break Test mechanic beyond Rout Tests.

docs.title: Leadership & Morale
docs.text:
Leadership in SHATTERED represents battlefield discipline, cohesion under pressure, and the ability of a warband to continue fighting in isolation.

Morale follows Mordheim conventions unless explicitly overridden.

There are no additional AoD status effects such as Pinning, Fear, or Reactions unless explicitly introduced elsewhere.

docs.title: 1. Rout Threshold
docs.text:
A warband must begin testing for Rout when 25% or more of its models are Out of Action.

The threshold is calculated based on the number of models in the warband at the start of the battle.

Models removed from play as casualties count toward this threshold.

docs.title: 2. Rout Tests
docs.text:
At the end of its turn, a warband that has reached the Rout threshold must take a Rout Test.

  • Roll 2D6.
  • If the result exceeds the Leader’s Leadership characteristic, the warband fails the test.
  • If failed, the battle ends immediately.
  • If passed, the warband continues fighting.

Rout Tests are taken once per turn while the threshold condition remains true.

docs.title: 3. Voluntary Withdrawal
docs.text:
If a warband has lost 25% or more of its models, it may voluntarily withdraw at the start of its Rout Phase.

  • Voluntary withdrawal ends the battle immediately.
  • No additional penalty is applied beyond the outcome of the battle itself.

docs.title: 4. Breaking from Combat
docs.text:
Breaking from combat is not a separate morale test.

Breaking is resolved under:

04-melee.md

There is no additional Break Test mechanic beyond the Rout system.

docs.title: 5. Zero Operational Models
docs.text: If a warband is reduced to zero Operational models, the battle ends immediately.

docs.title: Terrain & Verticality
docs.text:
SHATTERED is designed for dense, multi-level battlefields.

Vertical positioning, elevation advantage, and hazardous movement are central to gameplay.

Terrain interaction follows Mordheim conventions unless explicitly overridden.

docs.title: 1. Difficult Terrain
docs.text:
Difficult terrain reduces movement.

  • Moving through difficult terrain costs double movement.
  • A model may not Run through difficult terrain unless physically possible within its movement allowance.
  • Charges through difficult terrain must still legally reach base contact.

docs.title: 2. Climbing
docs.text:
Models may climb vertical surfaces where physically possible.

  • A model may attempt to climb up to its full Movement characteristic.
  • Climbing requires an Initiative test.
  • If the test is failed, the model falls (see Falling).

A model must be able to physically stand in the destination position.

docs.title: 3. Jumping
docs.text:
A model may leap across horizontal gaps.

  • A model may Jump up to a distance equal to its Initiative (I) in inches.
  • Jumping requires an Initiative test.
  • If the test is failed, the model falls.

Jumping is resolved during the Movement Phase.

docs.title: 4. Falling
docs.text:
If a model falls:

  • Measure the distance fallen.
  • The model suffers a hit following Mordheim falling rules.
  • Armour saves are taken as normal.
  • If the model survives, it is placed Prone (Knocked Down).

Falling may occur as a result of:

  • Failed climbing.
  • Failed jumping.
  • Being forced off a ledge.
  • Any rule that causes displacement from elevation.

docs.title: 5. Death from Above
docs.text:
Death from Above follows the Mordheim Diving Charge rules.

A model charging from a higher elevation may attempt a Diving Charge.

  • The model must declare a Charge.
  • The model must have sufficient movement to reach the target.
  • The model must pass an Initiative test.

If successful:

  • The charging model gains +1 to hit in the first round of combat.

If failed:

  • The model falls and suffers falling effects.

Death from Above represents a committed, high-risk vertical assault.

docs.title: 6. Elevation and Line of Sight
docs.text:
Line of sight is determined physically.

Models positioned at higher elevation:

  • May see over lower obstacles where physically appropriate.
  • Do not gain automatic defensive bonuses unless provided by terrain.

There are no abstract elevation modifiers beyond those defined above.

docs.title: Weapons & Gear Overview
docs.text:
SHATTERED uses Warhammer: Horus Heresy – Age of Darkness as the primary reference for:

  • Weapon profiles
  • Armour profiles
  • Wargear profiles

Unless explicitly overridden in SHATTERED, use the profile as printed.

This section describes how weapons and gear are interpreted and used within SHATTERED’s Mordheim-first structure.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
This section covers:

  • How to read and apply AoD weapon and armour profiles in SHATTERED
  • Restrictions and exclusions (e.g., Keywords not applying unless referenced)
  • Shared mechanical interpretations used across Shooting and Melee

Detailed in-battle procedures are defined in Core Rules:

  • Shooting procedure: docs/10-core-rules/03-shooting.md
  • Melee procedure: docs/10-core-rules/04-melee.md

Campaign access to equipment (what you can obtain, when, and how) is defined elsewhere.

docs.title: Rules Authority
docs.text:
• Mordheim governs structure and sequencing.
  • Age of Darkness governs weapon and gear statistics unless explicitly overridden.

The following AoD systems do not apply unless explicitly referenced in SHATTERED:

  • Reactions
  • Challenges
  • Keywords (unless explicitly referenced in SHATTERED)

All actions, targeting, and injury resolution occur at the model level.

docs.title: Firepower (FP)
docs.text: Weapons with a Firepower (FP) characteristic greater than 2 still count as a single shot for SHATTERED ammunition tracking purposes.

docs.title: Ammunition Scarcity Reference
docs.text:
Ammunition scarcity is resolved in the Shooting rules:

  • See docs/10-core-rules/03-shooting.md

Bolt Pistols are explicitly exempt from ammunition scarcity.

docs.title: Special Weapons Instability Reference
docs.text:
Special weapon instability (unmodified hit rolls of 1 triggering misfire) is resolved in the Shooting rules:

  • See docs/10-core-rules/03-shooting.md

docs.title: Change Log
docs.text: • 2026-02-13 — Initial draft

docs.title: Ranged Weapons
docs.text:
Ranged weapons in SHATTERED use the profiles printed in:

Warhammer: Horus Heresy – Age of Darkness (3rd Edition Liber books)

Unless explicitly overridden in SHATTERED, use the weapon’s:

  • Range
  • Strength
  • AP
  • Type
  • Firepower (FP)
  • Special rules

All shooting resolution follows the procedures defined in:

docs/10-core-rules/03-shooting.md

docs.title: 1. Weapon Types
docs.text: Weapon Types (e.g., Assault, Heavy, Rapid Fire) are interpreted using Age of Darkness definitions, except where explicitly modified by SHATTERED.

docs.title: 1.1 Assault Weapons
docs.text:
Assault weapons may be fired even if the model moved during its activation.

They are not subject to the –1 to hit penalty for moving.

All other shooting modifiers still apply.

docs.title: 2. Ammunition Scarcity (Standard Weapons)
docs.text:
At the start of each battle, roll 1D6 per ranged weapon to determine ammunition availability.

This roll applies only for the duration of that battle.

D6 Result 1 Unstable Supply — Any unmodified hit roll of 1 triggers a Weapon Misfire roll 2–5 The weapon may fire that many times during the battle 6 Unlimited shots for the battle
  • Weapons with Firepower (FP) greater than 2 still count as a single shot.
  • Bolt Pistols are not subject to ammunition scarcity.

Once a weapon has expended its available shots, it may not be fired again during that battle.

docs.title: 3. Weapon Misfire
docs.text:
When required to roll for Weapon Misfire, resolve the result using the Mordheim Blackpowder Weapon Misfire table.

This table is reproduced in:

docs/10-core-rules/03-shooting.md

The Misfire table is used identically and without modification.

docs.title: 4. Special Weapons
docs.text:
Special weapons use their printed Age of Darkness profiles, with modified ammunition rules.

At the start of each battle, roll 1D3 per Special Weapon to determine ammunition availability:

D3 Result 1 The weapon may fire once during the battle 2 The weapon may fire twice during the battle 3 The weapon may fire three times during the battle

Special weapons:

  • May never have unlimited shots.
  • Always roll on the Weapon Misfire table on any unmodified hit roll of 1.

This applies regardless of ammunition remaining.

Once a Special Weapon has expended its available shots, it may not be fired again during that battle.

docs.title: 5. Multiple Wounds
docs.text:
If a ranged weapon causes multiple wounds:

  • Resolve damage normally.
  • When rolling on the Injury Table, apply +1 to the Injury roll.

This modifier is applied after armour saves are resolved.

docs.title: 6. Targeting
docs.text:
All targeting is resolved at the model level.

  • There is no wound pooling.
  • There are no units.
  • Each shot targets a specific model.

Closest target restrictions and shooting modifiers are defined in:

docs/10-core-rules/03-shooting.md

docs.title: 7. Exclusions
docs.text:
The following Age of Darkness systems do not apply:

  • Reactions
  • Challenges
  • Keyword-based interactions (unless explicitly referenced in SHATTERED)

docs.title: Change Log
docs.text:
• 2026-02-13 — Initial draft
  • 2026-02-13 — Adjusted Special Weapon scarcity to D3 (never unlimited; always misfires on 1)

docs.title: Melee Weapons
docs.text:
Melee weapons in SHATTERED use the profiles printed in:

Warhammer: Horus Heresy – Age of Darkness (3rd Edition Liber books)

Unless explicitly overridden in SHATTERED, use the weapon’s:

  • Strength modifiers
  • AP
  • Special rules
  • Wound characteristics

Close combat resolution follows the procedure defined in:

docs/10-core-rules/04-melee.md

docs.title: 1. Weapon Profiles
docs.text:
All melee weapons use their printed Age of Darkness characteristics.

Special rules printed on a weapon apply unless:

  • They rely on Reactions,
  • They rely on Challenges,
  • They rely on Keywords not explicitly referenced in SHATTERED.

In such cases, ignore those elements.

docs.title: 2. Parry
docs.text:
The following grant Parry:

  • Swords
  • Combat Shields

A model may only use one Parry per combat opponent.

If a model is engaged with multiple enemies, it may Parry once against each opponent.

Parry follows Mordheim conventions:

  • The highest successful enemy hit may be forced to re-roll.

Parry does not stack against a single opponent.

docs.title: 3. Multiple Wounds
docs.text:
If a melee weapon causes multiple wounds:

  • Resolve damage normally.
  • When rolling on the Injury Table, apply +1 to the Injury roll.

This modifier is applied after armour saves are resolved.

docs.title: 4. Critical Hits
docs.text:
Critical hits in melee:

  • Bypass armour saves.

Critical resolution follows the procedure defined in:

docs/10-core-rules/04-melee.md

docs.title: 5. Engaged Combat
docs.text:
All melee combat is resolved at the model level.

  • There are no units.
  • There is no wound pooling.
  • A model engaged with multiple enemies may choose which engaged enemy to direct its attacks against.

Charging, initiative order, and combat sequencing are defined in:

docs/10-core-rules/04-melee.md

docs.title: 6. Exclusions
docs.text:
The following Age of Darkness systems do not apply:

  • Challenges
  • Reactions
  • Keyword-based conditional effects (unless explicitly referenced in SHATTERED)

docs.title: Change Log
docs.text: • 2026-02-13 — Initial draft

docs.title: Armour
docs.text:
Armour in SHATTERED uses the profiles printed in:

Warhammer: Horus Heresy – Age of Darkness (3rd Edition Liber books)

Unless explicitly overridden in SHATTERED, use the model’s printed Armour Save.

docs.title: 1. Armour Saves
docs.text:
When a model suffers a wound:

  1. Roll to hit.
  2. Roll to wound.
  3. Apply Armour Save.
  4. If the save fails, roll on the Injury Table.

Armour saves are modified by the attacking weapon’s AP characteristic as printed in Age of Darkness.

docs.title: 2. Critical Hits
docs.text:
Critical hits:

  • Bypass Armour Saves entirely.

If a hit is a Critical Hit, do not roll an Armour Save.

Resolve the Injury roll directly.

Critical resolution is defined in:

docs/10-core-rules/04-melee.md

docs.title: 3. Multiple Wounds
docs.text:
If a weapon causes multiple wounds:

  • Resolve armour saves normally.
  • If the wound proceeds to Injury resolution, apply +1 to the Injury roll.

docs.title: 4. Armour Degradation
docs.text:
Armour may become degraded through campaign injuries.

For example:

  • Armour Compromised: –1 to Armour Save until repaired.

Repair procedures are defined in:

docs/30-campaign/

Armour degradation never modifies a model’s base profile permanently unless explicitly stated.

docs.title: 5. No Additional Defensive Layers
docs.text:
The following Age of Darkness defensive systems do not apply unless explicitly referenced elsewhere in SHATTERED:

  • Reactions
  • Challenge-based defensive effects
  • Keyword-based defensive modifiers

Invulnerable saves or special defensive rules apply only if explicitly retained within SHATTERED.

docs.title: 6. Model-Level Resolution
docs.text:
All armour saves are resolved at the model level.

  • There is no wound pooling.
  • Each model rolls its own saves.
  • Casualties are applied individually.

docs.title: Change Log
docs.text: • 2026-02-13 — Initial draft

docs.title: Special Rules
docs.text: Special Rules associated with weapons as per AOD 3.0 rules are assummed to not be applicable in Shattered unless noted below (with or without modification).

docs.title: Breaching(X)
docs.text:
Breaching(X) lowers the die result required to score a Critical Hit. Critical Hits bypass the armour save. Normally, a Critical Hit is scored on a natural roll of 6 when rolling to wound. If a weapon has Breaching(X), a Critical Hit is instead scored on a natural roll of X or higher.

Examples: - Breaching (6) – Critical Hit on 5 or 6 - Breaching (5+) – Critical Hit on 4, 5, or 6 - Breaching (4+) – Critical Hit on 3, 4, 5, or 6

docs.title: Shred(X)
docs.text:
Shred(X) represents weapons that tear through flesh and internal systems, causing catastrophic damage when they strike cleanly.

If the attacker rolls a natural X or higher when rolling to wound, the hit Shreds the target.

A Shredding hit adds +1 to the Injury roll if the target is taken Out of Action.

The target may still take Armour Saves normally before any Injury roll is made.

docs.title: Warband Overview
docs.text:
A warband represents a fragmented Astartes force operating in isolation.

Warbands are constructed using Requisition Points (RP) and consist of individual models, not units.

This section defines how warbands are assembled, structured, equipped, and maintained between games.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
This section covers:

  • Starting Requisition values
  • Composition limits
  • Heroes and Henchmen structure
  • Model costs
  • Baseline equipment
  • Upgrade permissions and restrictions
  • Equipment exclusions
  • Roster requirements

Detailed in-battle procedures are defined in Core Rules.

Campaign advancement, injuries, and post-battle management are defined in Campaign sections.

docs.title: Structural Authority
docs.text:
• Warbands are not units.
  • No model benefits from unit-based scaling rules.
  • The Age of Darkness rule “For every five models in the unit…” does not apply in SHATTERED.
  • All rules operate at the individual model level.

docs.title: Heroes and Henchmen
docs.text:
Warbands are divided into:

  • Heroes
  • Henchmen

Heroes generate advancement and campaign progression benefits as defined in Campaign rules.

Henchmen operate as individual models and do not form units.

docs.title: Requisition Points (RP)
docs.text:
Requisition represents manpower, materiel, and logistical support available to an isolated force.

RP is used to:

  • Recruit models
  • Replace casualties
  • Purchase permitted upgrades

Starting RP values are defined in the Warband Creation section.

docs.title: Change Log
docs.text: • 2026-02-17 — Initial draft

docs.title: Warband Creation
docs.text:
Warbands are created using Requisition Points (RP).

Each model is purchased individually.

Warbands are not units and never qualify for unit-based rules or scaling unlocks.

docs.title: Starting Requisition
docs.text:
Recommended starting value:

  • 100 RP

All models and upgrades must be paid for using RP.

Unspent RP may be retained for campaign use.

docs.title: Composition Structure
docs.text:
Warbands are divided into:

  • Heroes
  • Henchmen

docs.title: Mandatory Model
docs.text:
• Exactly 1 Veteran Sergeant

A warband may not begin play without a Veteran Sergeant.

docs.title: Composition Limits
docs.text:
A warband may include:

Heroes - 1 Veteran Sergeant (mandatory) - 0–2 Veteran Marines

Henchmen - 0–3 Tactical Marines (10 RP each) - 0–3 Despoiler Marines (10 RP each) - 0–2 Breacher Marines (12 RP each) - 0–2 Tactical Support Marines (8 RP each)

These limits apply at all times.

The Age of Darkness scaling rule “For every five models in the unit…” does not apply in SHATTERED.

docs.title: Model Costs
docs.text:
Heroes - Veteran Sergeant — 15 RP - Veteran Marine — 15 RP

Henchmen - Tactical Marine — 10 RP - Despoiler Marine — 10 RP - Breacher Marine — 12 RP - Tactical Support Marine — 8 RP

RP must be paid in full when recruiting a model.

RP is not refunded unless explicitly stated elsewhere.

docs.title: Baseline Equipment
docs.text:
All models receive their default equipment as listed in the appropriate Age of Darkness Liber books at no additional RP cost.

Veteran Sergeants and Veteran Marines must begin with either:

  • A Bolter
  • A Bolt Pistol

They may not have both.

Exceptions:

  • Tactical Marines do not receive Bolt Pistols.
  • Breacher Marines do not receive Bolt Pistols.

Baseline equipment must be recorded on the roster.

docs.title: Veteran Sergeant
docs.text:
May select upgrades from:

  • Legion officer wargear
  • Legion combi-weapons

docs.title: Veteran Marines
docs.text:
May select upgrades from:

  • Sergeant melee weapons
  • Legion pistols

May upgrade to:

  • Astartes shotgun
  • Disintegrator rifle
  • Volkite charger

docs.title: Tactical Support Marines
docs.text:
May select one special weapon from the approved Special Weapons list.

No other upgrades permitted.

docs.title: Tactical Marines, Despoilers, and Breachers
docs.text:
• No upgrade options.
  • No additional wargear selections permitted.
  • May only use baseline equipment.

docs.title: Equipment Restrictions
docs.text:
The following are not permitted:

  • Heavy weapons
  • Terminator armour or Terminator weapons
  • Jump packs
  • Psykers
  • Techmarines
  • Apothecaries
  • Sponson, pintle, or vehicle-mounted weapons

Warbands never qualify for:

  • Unit-based unlocks
  • Scaling equipment allowances
  • “For every five models in the unit…” rules

docs.title: Roster Requirements
docs.text:
All models, equipment, and upgrades must be recorded on the warband roster.

A warband must remain within composition limits at all times.

docs.title: Campaign Overview
docs.text:
SHATTERED is designed for persistent campaign play.

Warbands accumulate injuries, salvage, experience, and reinforcements over multiple battles.

All post-battle resolution follows a mandatory sequence.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
This section governs all between-battle procedures, including:

  • Post-Battle Sequence
  • Injury Resolution
  • Resource Caches
  • Salvage
  • Exploration
  • Reinforcements
  • Experience and Advancement

These rules are resolved immediately after each battle.

docs.title: Structural Authority
docs.text:
• Consequence precedes recovery.
  • Recovery precedes opportunity.
  • Opportunity precedes growth.

The Post-Battle Sequence must be followed in order unless a scenario explicitly overrides it.

No campaign resource may be generated, spent, or resolved outside the Post-Battle Sequence unless explicitly stated.

docs.title: Campaign Principles
docs.text:
• Attrition is persistent.
  • Recovery is limited.
  • Reinforcement restores continuity, not strength.
  • Warbands do not scale through unit unlock mechanics.

All effects are recorded on the warband roster immediately.

docs.title: Post-Battle Sequence
docs.text:
Resolve the following steps in order after each battle.

This order is mandatory unless a scenario explicitly states otherwise.

docs.title: 1. Determine Battle Outcome
docs.text:
• Resolve scenario objectives.
  • Determine which warband controlled the field, if applicable.
  • A warband that Routed is considered to have lost.
  • Apply any scenario-specific post-battle effects.

docs.title: 2. Injury Resolution
docs.text:
• Roll on the appropriate Injury Table for every model taken Out of Action.
  • Apply all permanent and temporary injuries immediately.
  • Remove models that are killed or rendered unfit for service.

This step defines the warband’s true post-battle state.

docs.title: 3. Secure Resource Caches
docs.text:
• Determine which Resource Caches were secured.
  • Caches carried by surviving or withdrawing models are Secured.
  • Unsecured caches are lost.
  • Record the number of Secured Caches.

docs.title: 4. Resource Conversion
docs.text:
For each Secured Resource Cache:

  • Roll 1D3 and gain that many Requisition Points (RP).
  • Gain 1 Salvage.

RP gained during this step may not be spent during this sequence.

Record all totals on the warband roster.

docs.title: 5. Repair & Refit
docs.text:
• Spend Salvage to repair armour, weapons, or stabilise injuries.
  • Apply all effects immediately.
  • Salvage not spent is retained.

Salvage may only be spent during this step.

docs.title: 6. Exploration
docs.text:
• Roll Exploration dice as defined in the Exploration section.
  • Resolve all Exploration results.

If one or more Exploration dice show a 6, the warband may gain one Recruitment Opportunity, if eligible.

docs.title: 7. Recruitment
docs.text:
If a Recruitment Opportunity is available, the warband may:

  • Spend 5 RP and roll on the New Recruit Table, or
  • Decline the opportunity.

Unused Recruitment Opportunities expire at the end of this sequence.

docs.title: 8. Experience & Advancement
docs.text:
• Award Experience Points (XP).
  • Resolve advancements, if any.

Advancement follows the Mordheim Advance Roll system unless explicitly overridden.

docs.title: 9. Update Roster
docs.text:
• Record all injuries, repairs, RP totals, Salvage totals, XP gains, advancements, and roster changes.
  • Prepare the warband for the next battle.

docs.title: Injuries
docs.text:
Injuries represent lasting degradation: ruptured armour seals, concussive trauma, nerve damage, and cumulative attrition.

Roll on the appropriate Injury Table for each model taken Out of Action.

Injury Resolution occurs during Step 2 of the Post-Battle Sequence.

docs.title: Hero Injury Table
docs.text:
Roll 2D6 for each Hero taken Out of Action.

2D6 Result 2 Dead – Remove from roster. 3 Captured / Missing – Miss next battle. If the warband controls the field next game, return with Armour Compromised. Otherwise removed. 4 Multiple Trauma – Roll twice on this table (re-roll Dead). Apply both results. 5 Armour Compromised – –1 Armour Save until repaired (2 Salvage). 6 Weapon Damaged – One weapon gains a persistent Damaged condition until repaired (1 Salvage). 7 Full Recovery – No lasting effects. 8 Concussion – –1 Initiative next battle only. 9 Crippling Wound – –1 Movement permanently. 10 Oath of Moment – Choose a named foe; gain +1 Leadership for Rout tests and re-roll one failed To Hit per battle against them. 11 Bionic Replacement Needed – –1 WS until repaired or stabilised. 12 Hardened by Fire – Gain +1 XP immediately and re-roll the next Advance roll.

docs.title: Henchman Injury Table
docs.text:
Roll 2D6 for each Henchman taken Out of Action.

2D6 Result 2 Dead – Remove from roster. 3 Lost in the Ruins – Miss next battle. If the warband wins next game, return with No Weaponry. Otherwise removed. 4 Deep Wounds – Miss next battle. Return with Armour Compromised. 5 Armour Compromised – –1 Armour Save until repaired. 6 Weapon Damaged – One weapon becomes Damaged until repaired. 7 Full Recovery – No lasting effects. 8 Shaken – –1 Leadership next battle only. 9 Battle Scar – –1 Initiative permanently. 10 Eye Damage – –1 BS until repaired or stabilised. 11 Machine-Flesh – –1 Movement next battle only, then recovers. 12 Veteran Instincts – Gain +1 XP immediately.

docs.title: Injury Persistence
docs.text:
• Permanent effects remain until repaired or otherwise resolved.
  • Temporary effects expire as stated.
  • Models removed from the roster are permanently lost.

All injury results must be recorded immediately.

docs.title: Exploration
docs.text:
Exploration represents post-battle reconnaissance and recovery.

Exploration is resolved during Step 6 of the Post-Battle Sequence.

Exploration follows a Mordheim-style duplicate system.

docs.title: Exploration Dice
docs.text:
Roll Exploration dice as follows:

  • Roll 1D6 for each surviving Hero.
  • Roll +1D6 if the warband controlled the field.

Models taken Out of Action do not generate Exploration dice.

If more than three dice are rolled, retain only the three highest dice for all purposes.

docs.title: Resolving Exploration
docs.text:
After rolling:

  1. Retain up to three highest dice.
  2. Check the retained dice for duplicates.
  3. If any duplicates are present, resolve the Duplicate Table.
  4. If all retained dice are unique, resolve the Total Table.

Duplicate results override the Total Table.

If more than one duplicate set exists, resolve each set separately.

A result of three 1s or three 6s is resolved as a triple, not as a total of 3 or 18.

docs.title: Total Table (All Unique Dice)
docs.text:
Add the three retained dice.

Possible totals: 6–15.

Total Result 6 Scorched Rubble — Gain 1 Salvage. 7 Broken Armour Plates — Gain 2 Salvage. 8 Recovered Ammunition — Gain 1D3 RP. 9 Battlefield Debris — Gain 1 Salvage and 1 RP. 10 Partial Supply Crate — Gain 1D3 RP and 1 Salvage. 11 Field Medicae Station — Re-roll one Injury result from this sequence (must accept second result). Gain 1 Salvage. 12 Sealed Locker — Gain 1D3 RP and 2 Salvage. 13 Strategic Data Core — Gain 2 RP. One Hero gains +1 XP. 14 Hardened Supply Vault — Gain 2D3 RP and 1 Salvage. 15 Hidden Arsenal — Gain 2D3 RP and 2 Salvage.

docs.title: Duplicate Table
docs.text:
Resolve once for each duplicate set among the retained dice.

Use the duplicated value to determine the result.

docs.title: Double 1 – Collapsed Substructure
docs.text: Gain 1 Salvage.

docs.title: Double 2 – Scattered Munitions
docs.text: Gain 1D3 RP.

docs.title: Double 3 – Damaged Vox Relay
docs.text: Gain 1 Salvage. Re-roll one retained Exploration die before resolving further duplicates.

docs.title: Double 4 – Fallen Legionary
docs.text: Gain 2 Salvage.

docs.title: Double 5 – Armoury Fragments
docs.text: Gain 1D3 RP and 1 Salvage.

docs.title: Double 6 – Legion Distress Signal
docs.text: Gain 1 Recruitment Opportunity.

docs.title: Triple 1 – Rad-Saturated Vault
docs.text: One random participating model suffers a Strength 4 hit. Gain 1 Salvage.

docs.title: Triple 2 – Munitorum Depot
docs.text: Gain 2D3 RP.

docs.title: Triple 3 – Tactical Intelligence Cache
docs.text: Gain 2 RP. One Hero gains +1 XP.

docs.title: Triple 4 – Secure Armoury
docs.text: Gain 2 Salvage and 1D3 RP.

docs.title: Triple 5 – Mechanicum Stockpile
docs.text: Gain 3 Salvage.

docs.title: Triple 6 – Shattered Legion Survivor
docs.text: Gain 1 Recruitment Opportunity. Gain 1 Salvage.

docs.title: Quadruple (Any)
docs.text: Gain 2D3 RP and 2 Salvage.

docs.title: Five of a Kind (Any)
docs.text: Gain 3D3 RP and 3 Salvage. Gain 1 Recruitment Opportunity.

docs.title: Recruitment Trigger
docs.text:
If any retained die shows a natural 6:

  • Gain one Recruitment Opportunity, if eligible.
  • Only one Recruitment Opportunity may be gained per post-battle sequence.

Recruitment is resolved during Step 7 of the Post-Battle Sequence.

docs.title: Restrictions
docs.text:
Exploration may not:

  • Add models directly
  • Grant wargear directly
  • Override composition limits

All results are applied immediately.

docs.title: Salvage & Requisition
docs.text:
Salvage and Requisition Points (RP) represent the material condition of a warband.

Both are generated during the Post-Battle Sequence.

Neither may be generated or spent outside that sequence unless explicitly stated.

docs.title: Requisition Points (RP)
docs.text:
Requisition Points represent manpower, munitions, and logistical support.

RP is used to:

  • Recruit new models
  • Replace casualties
  • Pay upgrade costs
  • Pay Recruitment Opportunity costs

RP gained during Step 4 (Resource Conversion) may not be spent during the same Post-Battle Sequence.

RP gained during Exploration may be spent during Step 7 (Recruitment), unless explicitly restricted.

Unspent RP is retained between battles.

RP is never refunded when a model is removed from the roster unless explicitly stated.

docs.title: Resource Caches
docs.text:
Resource Caches are the primary guaranteed source of RP and Salvage.

For each Secured Resource Cache:

  • Roll 1D3 and gain that many RP.
  • Gain 1 Salvage.

Unsecured caches are lost.

Resource Caches never grant models or equipment directly.

docs.title: Salvage
docs.text:
Salvage represents recoverable materiel: armour plating, munitions, machine components, and battlefield remnants.

Salvage may only be spent during Step 5: Repair & Refit.

Unspent Salvage is retained between battles.

Salvage may never:

  • Improve characteristics
  • Purchase weapons
  • Recruit models
  • Override composition limits

docs.title: Spending Salvage
docs.text:
Salvage may be spent only during Step 5 of the Post-Battle Sequence.

Permitted uses:

  • Repair Armour Compromised — 2 Salvage
  • Repair Damaged Weapon — 1 Salvage
  • Re-equip Weaponless Recruit — 1 Salvage
  • Stabilise Injury — 1 Salvage
  • Re-roll one non-permanent Injury result
  • Must accept the second result

All repairs take effect immediately.

Salvage may not be spent after Recruitment or Advancement steps begin.

docs.title: Restrictions
docs.text:
Salvage and RP:

  • Do not stack beyond recorded totals.
  • Do not grant scaling benefits.
  • Do not activate unit-based mechanics.

All gains and expenditures must be recorded immediately on the warband roster.

docs.title: Recruitment
docs.text:
Recruitment restores losses within a warband.

Recruitment does not increase maximum composition limits.

Recruitment is resolved during Step 7 of the Post-Battle Sequence.

docs.title: Recruitment Opportunities
docs.text:
A Recruitment Opportunity is generated if:

  • Any retained Exploration die shows a natural 6.

Only one Recruitment Opportunity may be gained per Post-Battle Sequence.

A Recruitment Opportunity:

  • Expires at the end of the current Post-Battle Sequence.
  • Requires an open warband slot.
  • Does not stack.

If no open slot exists, the opportunity is lost.

docs.title: Cost
docs.text:
To recruit:

  • Select an eligible model type with an open slot.
  • Pay the full RP cost of that model as listed in Warband Creation.
  • Consume the Recruitment Opportunity.

If sufficient RP is not available, the opportunity expires.

docs.title: Recruitment Procedure
docs.text:
After paying the model’s full RP cost:

  1. Add the model to the roster.
  2. Roll 2D6 on the Recruitment Table.
  3. Apply the result immediately.
  4. Roll on the Allegiance Table (Draft).
  5. Record all effects.

Recruits:

  • Begin with 0 XP unless otherwise stated.
  • Must follow all Warband Creation equipment restrictions.
  • May not exceed composition limits.

Recruitment never bypasses:

  • Model caps
  • Equipment restrictions
  • Upgrade limitations

docs.title: Recruitment Table (2D6)
docs.text: 2D6 Result 2 Deranged Survivor — The recruit is unstable. Gains +1 Attack in the first round of combat in each battle. At the start of each battle, roll 1D6: on a 1, the model must move toward the nearest visible enemy in its first activation if able. 3 Armour Scarred — Begins with Armour Compromised. 4 Weaponless Arrival — Begins with No Weaponry. Must be re-equipped using Salvage. 5 Shaken Survivor — –1 Leadership next battle only. 6 Line Legionary — No additional effects. 7 Recovered Brother — No additional effects. 8 Disciplined Fighter — Gain +1 XP immediately. 9 Proven Under Fire — Gain +1 XP. 10 Recovered Wargear — Gain 1 Salvage. 11 Veteran of Attrition — Gain +2 XP. 12 Unbroken Astartes — Gain +1 XP and +1 Leadership for Rout tests while this model remains operational.

docs.title: Allegiance & Cohesion (Draft)
docs.text:
Status: Draft

After resolving the Recruitment Table, roll 2D6 on the Allegiance Table.

This represents fractured loyalties, inter-Legion mistrust, and the instability of shattered forces.

Apply the result immediately.

docs.title: Allegiance Table (2D6)
docs.text: 2D6 Result 2 Open Hostility — The recruit begins with –1 Leadership permanently. The warband must re-roll its first successful Rout test each battle while this model is operational. 3 Distrusted — The recruit may not benefit from Leadership re-rolls from the Veteran Sergeant. 4 Tense Alliance — No mechanical effect. Narrative only. 5 Uneasy Cohesion — –1 Leadership in the first battle only. 6 Conditional Loyalty — No additional effect. 7 Accepted into the Line — No additional effect. 8 Earned Respect — Gain +1 XP immediately. 9 Trusted Brother — While operational, the warband may re-roll one failed Rout test per campaign. 10 Shared Oath — The recruit and one Hero both gain +1 XP. 11 Battle-Bound — While within 6" of the Veteran Sergeant, the recruit gains +1 Leadership. 12 Oath-Sworn to the Shattered — The recruit ignores the first failed Rout test they would suffer each battle.

docs.title: Restrictions
docs.text:
Allegiance results:

  • Do not alter composition limits.
  • Do not grant equipment.
  • Do not override core Leadership rules unless explicitly stated.

All effects are recorded immediately.

docs.title: Advancement
docs.text:
Advancement represents battlefield adaptation and accumulated experience.

Advancement is resolved during Step 8 of the Post-Battle Sequence.

All models track Experience Points (XP) individually.

Warbands are not units and never gain shared advancements.

docs.title: Experience Points (XP)
docs.text:
Models gain XP from the following sources:

  • Survive the battle — +1 XP
  • Take an enemy Out of Action — +1 XP
  • Scenario contribution — +1 XP (maximum once per battle)
  • Warband controls the field — Veteran Sergeant gains +1 XP

A model may gain a maximum of +3 XP per battle.

XP is recorded immediately.

docs.title: Advancement Roll
docs.text:
When a model reaches an advancement threshold:

  • Roll 3D6.
  • Apply the result from the Advancement Table.

If the result cannot be applied, re-roll.

Results of 3, 12, or 18 instead generate a roll on the Characteristic Table (1D6).

Each model may only gain each characteristic increase once ever.

docs.title: Advancement Table (3D6)
docs.text: 3D6 Result 4 Relentless Advance — May move and fire non-assault weapons without penalty. 5 Close-Quarters Killer — Re-roll one failed To Wound roll in melee per battle. 6 Steady Under Fire — Ignore the first failed Ammo roll each battle. 7 Oath of Moment — Nominate one enemy model at the start of the battle. Re-roll one failed To Hit roll per round against that model. 8 Grim Determination — May ignore being Knocked Down once per battle. 9 Brutal Charge — +1 Strength in the first round of a charge. 10 Heart of the Legion — If this model did not move in its activation this round, it gains +1 FP when firing. 11 Precision Fire — Model does not need to target the closest model when firing. 13 Battlefield Calculus — Once per battle, after both sides have rolled for activation priority, you may re-roll your result. 14 Fearless Aspect — Causes Fear. 15 Executioner — If this model takes an enemy Out of Action in melee, gain +1 XP (maximum once per battle). 16 Battlefield Predator — +1 Attack in the first round of combat. 17 Iron Resolve — Once per battle, this model may ignore the effects of being Stunned. Treat the result as Knocked Down instead.

docs.title: Characteristic Table (1D6)
docs.text:
Roll 1D6 on a result of 3, 12, or 18 on the Advancement Roll.

Each characteristic may only be increased once per model.

1D6 Result 1 +1 Weapon Skill 2 +1 Ballistic Skill 3 +1 Initiative 4 +1 Leadership 5 +1 Attack 6 +1 Wound

If a rolled characteristic has already been increased for this model, re-roll.

docs.title: Restrictions
docs.text:
Advancement may not:

  • Grant additional wargear
  • Bypass composition limits
  • Remove permanent injuries unless explicitly stated
  • Activate unit-based mechanics

All results are applied immediately and recorded on the roster.

docs.title: Post-Battle Sequence
docs.text:
Resolve in this order after every battle.

Step Action 1 Determine Outcome — Resolve objectives. Routed warband loses. 2 Injury Resolution — Roll on Injury Table for every model taken Out of Action. 3 Secure Resource Caches — Caches held by surviving/withdrawing models are Secured. Others are lost. 4 Resource Conversion — Per Secured Cache: roll 1D3 → gain that many RP. Gain 1 Salvage. RP from this step cannot be spent this sequence. 5 Repair & Refit — Spend Salvage now. Unspent Salvage is retained. 6 Exploration — Roll Exploration dice. Resolve results. 7 Recruitment — If a Recruitment Opportunity is available, spend 5 RP and roll on the New Recruit Table, or decline. 8 Experience & Advancement — Award XP. Resolve advancements. 9 Update Roster — Record everything. Prepare for next battle.

docs.title: Injury Resolution
docs.text: Roll for every model taken Out of Action. Apply results immediately.

docs.title: Hero Injury Table — roll 2D6
docs.text: 2D6 Result 2 Dead — Remove from roster. 3 Captured/Missing — Miss next battle. Returns with Armour Compromised if warband controls field next game; otherwise removed. 4 Multiple Trauma — Roll twice (re-roll Dead). Apply both. 5 Armour Compromised — –1 Armour Save until repaired (2 Salvage). 6 Weapon Damaged — One weapon gains Damaged until repaired (1 Salvage). 7 Full Recovery — No lasting effects. 8 Concussion — –1 Initiative next battle only. 9 Crippling Wound — –1 Movement permanently. 10 Oath of Moment — Choose a named foe; +1 Ld on Rout tests and re-roll one failed To Hit per battle against them. 11 Bionic Replacement Needed — –1 WS until repaired or stabilised. 12 Hardened by Fire — +1 XP immediately. Re-roll the next Advance roll.

docs.title: Henchman Injury Table — roll 2D6
docs.text: 2D6 Result 2 Dead — Remove from roster. 3 Lost in the Ruins — Miss next battle. Returns with No Weaponry if warband wins next game; otherwise removed. 4 Deep Wounds — Miss next battle. Returns with Armour Compromised. 5 Armour Compromised — –1 Armour Save until repaired. 6 Weapon Damaged — One weapon becomes Damaged until repaired. 7 Full Recovery — No lasting effects. 8 Shaken — –1 Leadership next battle only. 9 Battle Scar — –1 Initiative permanently. 10 Eye Damage — –1 BS until repaired or stabilised. 11 Machine-Flesh — –1 Movement next battle only, then recovers. 12 Veteran Instincts — +1 XP immediately.

docs.title: Salvage Spending
docs.text:
Salvage may only be spent during Step 5: Repair & Refit.

Action Cost Repair Armour Compromised 2 Salvage Repair Damaged Weapon 1 Salvage Re-equip Weaponless Recruit 1 Salvage Stabilise Injury (re-roll one non-permanent result; must accept second) 1 Salvage

Salvage cannot improve characteristics, purchase weapons, or recruit models.

docs.title: Exploration
docs.text:
Resolved during Step 6.

Roll Exploration dice: - 1D6 per surviving Hero. - +1D6 if the warband controlled the field. - If more than 3 dice are rolled, retain only the three highest.

Resolving results: 1. Check retained dice for duplicates. 2. If duplicates exist → use Duplicate Table (overrides Total Table). 3. If all unique → add the three dice and use Total Table. 4. If any retained die shows a natural 6 → gain 1 Recruitment Opportunity (one per sequence).

docs.title: Total Table (all unique dice — add results)
docs.text: Total Result 6 Scorched Rubble — 1 Salvage. 7 Broken Armour Plates — 2 Salvage. 8 Recovered Ammunition — 1D3 RP. 9 Battlefield Debris — 1 Salvage, 1 RP. 10 Partial Supply Crate — 1D3 RP, 1 Salvage. 11 Field Medicae Station — Re-roll one Injury result (must accept second). 1 Salvage. 12 Sealed Locker — 1D3 RP, 2 Salvage. 13 Strategic Data Core — 2 RP. One Hero +1 XP. 14 Hardened Supply Vault — 2D3 RP, 1 Salvage. 15 Hidden Arsenal — 2D3 RP, 2 Salvage.

docs.title: Duplicate Table
docs.text: Roll Result Double 1 Collapsed Substructure — 1 Salvage. Double 2 Scattered Munitions — 1D3 RP. Double 3 Damaged Vox Relay — 1 Salvage. Re-roll one retained die before resolving further duplicates. Double 4 Fallen Legionary — 2 Salvage. Double 5 Armoury Fragments — 1D3 RP, 1 Salvage. Double 6 Legion Distress Signal — 1 Recruitment Opportunity. Triple 1 Rad-Saturated Vault — One random model suffers S4 hit. 1 Salvage. Triple 2 Munitorum Depot — 2D3 RP. Triple 3 Tactical Intelligence Cache — 2 RP. One Hero +1 XP. Triple 4 Secure Armoury — 2 Salvage, 1D3 RP. Triple 5 Mechanicum Stockpile — 3 Salvage. Triple 6 Shattered Legion Survivor — 1 Recruitment Opportunity, 1 Salvage. Quadruple 2D3 RP, 2 Salvage. Five of a Kind 3D3 RP, 3 Salvage, 1 Recruitment Opportunity.

docs.title: Recruitment
docs.text:
Resolved during Step 7. Requires a Recruitment Opportunity and an open warband slot.

  • Pay the full RP cost of the model type.
  • Roll 2D6 on the Recruitment Table.
  • Roll 2D6 on the Allegiance Table.
  • Apply all results immediately.

Recruits begin with 0 XP. Must follow all Warband Creation equipment restrictions.

docs.title: Recruitment Table — roll 2D6
docs.text: 2D6 Result 2 Deranged Survivor — +1 Attack in first round of combat each battle. Roll 1D6 at battle start: on 1, must move toward nearest visible enemy first activation. 3 Armour Scarred — Begins with Armour Compromised. 4 Weaponless Arrival — Begins with No Weaponry. Must be re-equipped with Salvage. 5 Shaken Survivor — –1 Leadership next battle only. 6 Line Legionary — No additional effects. 7 Recovered Brother — No additional effects. 8 Disciplined Fighter — +1 XP immediately. 9 Proven Under Fire — +1 XP. 10 Recovered Wargear — 1 Salvage. 11 Veteran of Attrition — +2 XP. 12 Unbroken Astartes — +1 XP and +1 Ld on Rout tests while operational.

docs.title: Allegiance Table — roll 2D6
docs.text: 2D6 Result 2 Open Hostility — –1 Ld permanently. Warband must re-roll first successful Rout test each battle while this model is operational. 3 Distrusted — Cannot benefit from Ld re-rolls from Veteran Sergeant. 4 Tense Alliance — Narrative only. 5 Uneasy Cohesion — –1 Ld first battle only. 6 Conditional Loyalty — No additional effect. 7 Accepted into the Line — No additional effect. 8 Earned Respect — +1 XP immediately. 9 Trusted Brother — Warband may re-roll one failed Rout test per campaign while this model is operational. 10 Shared Oath — Recruit and one Hero both gain +1 XP. 11 Battle-Bound — +1 Ld while within 6" of the Veteran Sergeant. 12 Oath-Sworn to the Shattered — Ignores the first failed Rout test each battle.

docs.title: XP Awards (per battle)
docs.text:
Condition XP Survive the battle +1 Take an enemy Out of Action +1 Scenario contribution +1 (max once per battle) Warband controls the field (Veteran Sergeant only) +1

Maximum +3 XP per model per battle.

docs.title: Advancement Roll
docs.text:
When a model reaches an advancement threshold, roll 3D6 on the Advancement Table.

  • Results of 3, 12, or 18 → roll 1D6 on the Characteristic Table instead.
  • If a result cannot be applied, re-roll.
  • Each characteristic increase may only be gained once per model.

docs.title: Advancement Table — roll 3D6
docs.text: 3D6 Result 4 Relentless Advance — May move and fire non-Assault weapons without penalty. 5 Close-Quarters Killer — Re-roll one failed To Wound in melee per battle. 6 Steady Under Fire — Ignore the first failed Ammo roll each battle. 7 Oath of Moment — Nominate one enemy at battle start. Re-roll one failed To Hit per round against that model. 8 Grim Determination — May ignore being Knocked Down once per battle. 9 Brutal Charge — +1 Strength in the first round of a charge. 10 Heart of the Legion — If this model did not move this activation, +1 FP when firing. 11 Precision Fire — Does not need to target the closest model when firing. 13 Battlefield Calculus — Once per battle, after both sides roll for activation priority, may re-roll your result. 14 Fearless Aspect — Causes Fear. 15 Executioner — If this model takes an enemy Out of Action in melee, gain +1 XP (max once per battle). 16 Battlefield Predator — +1 Attack in the first round of combat. 17 Iron Resolve — Once per battle, ignore Stunned. Treat as Knocked Down instead.

docs.title: Characteristic Table — roll 1D6
docs.text:
1D6 Result 1 +1 Weapon Skill 2 +1 Ballistic Skill 3 +1 Initiative 4 +1 Leadership 5 +1 Attack 6 +1 Wound

If the rolled characteristic has already been increased for this model, re-roll.

docs.title: Sequence of Play
docs.text:
Phase Summary 1. Recovery Stunned → Knocked Down. Resolve start-of-turn effects. 2. Movement Move, Run, Charge, Jump, or Stand Up. 3. Shooting Eligible models resolve ranged attacks. 4. Close Combat All Engaged models fight. 5. Rout Check Rout threshold. Take Rout Test if required.

Players alternate activating their warband during each phase. No reactions. No interrupts.

docs.title: Movement
docs.text:
Action Distance Restrictions Normal Move M Cannot move through models or impassable terrain. Run M + I Cannot shoot. Cannot be Engaged at start of activation. Charge M + I Must reach base contact. Strikes first if successful.
  • Pre-measuring is permitted.
  • A model that cannot legally complete a move stops at the furthest legal point.

docs.title: Eligibility
docs.text: A model may shoot if it did not Run, did not Charge, and is not Engaged. Assault weapons may always fire unless otherwise prohibited.

docs.title: To-Hit Modifiers
docs.text:
Condition Modifier Firing model moved (non-Assault weapon) –1 to hit Target beyond half range –1 to hit Soft cover (partially obscured, or within 1" of another model) –1 to hit Hard cover (mostly obscured by solid terrain) –2 to hit

Modifiers stack. A model that cannot be seen cannot be targeted.

docs.title: Ammunition
docs.text:
Roll at the start of battle, once per weapon.

Boltguns — roll 1D6:

D6 Result 1 Unstable Supply — unmodified hit roll of 1 triggers Misfire. 2–5 That many shots for the battle. 6 Unlimited shots.

Special Weapons — roll 1D3:

D3 Result 1 One shot. 2 Two shots. 3 Three shots.
  • Bolt Pistols are not subject to ammunition scarcity.
  • Weapons with FP > 2 still count as a single shot.
  • Any unmodified hit roll of 1 with a Special Weapon triggers a Misfire.

docs.title: Misfire Table — roll 1D6
docs.text: D6 Result 1 BOOM! S4 hit on shooter (no crit possible). Weapon destroyed. 2 Jammed. Weapon useless for the rest of the battle. 3 Phut. Cannot fire this weapon next turn. 4–5 Click. Fails to fire, no further effect. 6 KA-BOOM! Hits intended target at +1 Strength.

docs.title: Charging
docs.text:
• Declare Charge → move up to M + I → must reach base contact.
  • Charging model strikes first in the first round of combat.
  • A model may not Charge if it began its activation Engaged.

docs.title: Shield Bash (Boarding Shield only)
docs.text:
May be declared if the charger would reach base contact with more than 2" of movement remaining.

Result Effect Charger wins Push target up to 2" directly away. Target falls if displaced off a ledge; otherwise Knocked Down. Charger retains strike priority. Tie or charger loses Shield Bash fails. Defender strikes first.

docs.title: Combat Sequence
docs.text:
1. Determine strike order (chargers first).
  2. Roll To Hit (WS vs WS).
  3. Roll To Wound.
  4. Resolve Armour Saves.

  5. Roll on Injury Table for each unsaved wound.

  6. Critical hits bypass armour saves.

  7. No wound pooling. Each model resolves attacks individually.

docs.title: Injury
docs.text:
Roll 1D6 per unsaved wound:

D6 Result 1–2 Knocked Down 3–4 Stunned 5–6 Out of Action

Multi-wound models: Ignore the first Out of Action result — instead lose one Wound. At 0 Wounds, Out of Action results apply normally. Weapons causing multiple wounds: +1 to Injury roll.

Status effects: - Knocked Down — may stand during Movement Phase. - Stunned — becomes Knocked Down at start of next Recovery Phase. - Out of Action — removed from play. Counts toward Rout threshold.

docs.title: Rout
docs.text:
• Rout threshold: 25% or more of starting models are Out of Action.
  • At the end of each turn the threshold is met, take a Rout Test.
  • Roll 2D6 — if result exceeds the Leader's Ld, the warband routs. Battle ends immediately.
  • A warband that has reached the threshold may voluntarily withdraw at the start of its Rout Phase with no additional penalty.

docs.title: Terrain & Verticality
docs.text:
Action Test On Failure Climb (up to M") Initiative test Model falls Jump (up to I") Initiative test Model falls Death from Above (Diving Charge) Initiative test Model falls

Death from Above (success): +1 to hit in the first round of combat.

Falling: Model suffers a hit (Mordheim falling rules). Armour saves apply. Survivor is placed Knocked Down.

Difficult terrain: Costs double movement. Cannot Run through difficult terrain unless it fits within the movement allowance.
